LAST_DONE: 2026-05-19 Rule 16 cleanup smoke pass (Phase 7 follow-up) — Tier 2 single-session Opus 4.7 controller (~110K context across the full pass given multi-tool browser automation overhead — Playwright MCP browser_snapshot + browser_evaluate + ctx_execute SQL probes + git operations). Driver: Playwright MCP with system Chrome (user installed google-chrome-stable via Google's apt repo since Playwright MCP defaults to /opt/google/chrome/chrome, not the Playwright bundled chromium). Fixtures: `prisma migrate reset --force --skip-generate` to drop+remigrate clean DB, then `pnpm db:seed` (required user-shared WEBMASTER_PASSWORD via line-selection from CREDENTIALS.md), then manual one-shot tsx fixture script (`/tmp/yelli-fixtures.ts`) to create 1 additional org `evil` + 4 test users in system org (alice/bob/carol/david, all `host` role, password `TestPassword12345678AB`, bcrypt cost 12) + 1 user in evil org (mallory) + 3 additional departments (Sales/Support/Front Desk, joining Reception) + bindings (alice→Reception, bob→Sales, david→Front Desk AND Support multi-binding, carol→unbound). PASS RESULTS (6): #8e Socket.IO handshake (curl → HTTP 200 with sid + websocket upgrade + 25s/20s ping config + CORS for :43512; browser session via SocketProvider connects + id verified via React fiber introspection), #10 session:invalidated (bumping security_version via direct SQL triggered redirect to /login in ~120s — note worst case is 2x the 60s loop interval, not the ≤60s the .whatsnext smoke description claims; sign-out alone does NOT trigger session:invalidated, only security_version/role/status changes do), #4 forgot-password (non-enumerating message + MailHog email with reset link + 1hr expiry + reset → new password → login works), #11 presence engine multi-tab (presence:snapshot captured with carol's id appearing once despite 2 sockets open in tabs A+B — multi-tab coalescing verified at server roster level; all 5 expected listeners attached: presence:snapshot=1, presence:user=1, call:active=1, call:active-snapshot=1, session:invalidated=1; closing tab B did not disconnect tab A — isLast=false path verified), #12 Speed Dial green-dot (alice signs in → Reception bound to alice goes "online"+clickable; Sales/Support/Front Desk bound to non-signed-in bob/david remain "offline"+disabled; React fiber state confirmed useUserPresence's onlineSet contains alice's id; minor caveat: first post-sign-in render showed Reception OFFLINE briefly then corrected after socket.disconnect+connect probe — possible initial-mount race, not consistently reproducible), #13 admin-binding-ui (sidebar Dashboard/Departments/Users/Billing/Reports/Settings/Super Admin renders for webmaster; departments table shows all 4 fixtures with bindings; Default user combobox opens with options; non-admin alice → redirected from /admin to /app). PARTIAL (1): #3 /app/meetings/new (form + creation + LiveKit token mint all PASS after env fix; WebRTC peer connection fails with "could not establish pc connection" — coturn restarting [255], blocks all in-call testing). BLOCKED (5): #14 in-call yellow-dot + #15 incoming-call dialog + #16 dept-filter routing — all need (a) 2+ distinct user sessions concurrently which Playwright MCP single-context cookie-sharing cannot provide AND (b) working WebRTC which coturn blocks; #7c subdomain redirect — /t/{slug}/* dev URL pattern always returns Next.js 404 because no route handlers exist under apps/web/src/app/t/ and middleware doesn't rewrite to strip the prefix; #14/#15/#16 underlying code IS covered at unit-test level by Phase 7 #14 (16 cases), #15 (16 cases), #16 (7 cases) — manual e2e smoke deferred until multi-browser rig + working coturn available. ONE CODE FIX SHIPPED INLINE: discovered apps/web/src/env.ts schema declared `LIVEKIT_WS_URL` but every .env.* + every compose file uses `LIVEKIT_URL` → `mintLiveKitToken`'s wsUrl was always undefined → `meetings.getJoinToken` returned 503 on EVERY call attempt. Five-line rename in env.ts + one in lib/livekit/client.ts on branch `fix/livekit-url-env-rename` (commit `417ed97`); two-stage review PASS; squash-merged to main as `f9f88bf`; pnpm test 167/167 ✓, typecheck/lint/build all clean.

NEXT: 4 follow-up tickets queued from Rule 16 cleanup findings + the prior Phase 7 #17 backlog:
  (t-slug-dev-routes-broken) — Add either Next.js route handlers under apps/web/src/app/t/[slug]/(app|admin|superadmin)/ that re-render the parent equivalents OR (preferred) a middleware rewrite that strips /t/{slug} after extracting the tenant slug to x-tenant-slug header. Tier 1. Currently /t/{slug}/anything always 404s; unit tests for buildTenantRedirectUrl pass because they only verify URL strings, never route serving.
  (admin-users-list-tenant-scope) — Verify whether `admin.users.list` properly enforces tenant scoping for plain `tenant_admin` users (without `is_super_admin=true`). During Rule 16 #13 smoke, signed-in webmaster (tenant_admin + is_super_admin=true) saw Mallory (in `evil` org) in /admin/departments user picker dropdown. May be intentional super-admin visibility — but security.md §SUPERADMIN forbids "inline if (isSuperadmin) skip tenant filter" — so either the L6 tenant guard correctly skips for super-admins (intentional) OR there's a leak. Test: create a plain tenant_admin (is_super_admin=false) + verify they only see same-org users in the dropdown. Tier 1 if intentional (just add docs/test), Tier 2 if leak (refactor to platformPrisma per security.md guidance).

Blocks all in-call manual smoke (#14/#15/#16). Tier 1 (fill credential, restart container).

LESSONS_ADDED (3 this session):
  🔴 [[livekit-env-name-mismatch]] — `LIVEKIT_WS_URL` in env.ts vs `LIVEKIT_URL` in every env/compose file caused silent 503 on every meeting/call. Lesson: any cross-file identifier rename MUST be applied to ALL source-of-truth files in one PR; add Rule 16 follow-up: run pnpm dev + curl critical tRPC endpoints to catch latent 503s with generic try/catch messages.
  🔴 [[t-slug-dev-routes-broken]] — /t/{slug}/* dev URL pattern has no Next.js route handlers under apps/web/src/app/t/; middleware extracts slug + redirects but never rewrites the path; tenant-redirect unit tests pass because they only verify URL strings. Two fix paths: route handlers OR middleware rewrite (prefer rewrite).
  🟤 [[rule-16-cleanup-2026-05-19]] — Rule 16 cleanup smoke pass findings + locked decision: Playwright MCP single-context cookie-sharing limits multi-user smoke testing; tickets touching multi-user realtime flows MUST have handler-level unit coverage + queue manual e2e smoke as Rule 16 follow-up when a multi-browser rig is available.

TIER_CLASSIFICATION: 2 — moderate. Per memory-governance §1: scope was effectively 1 module (env naming fix) + 12 smoke verifications across multiple modules. Score: (10 files inspected × 2.5) + (3 modules touched × 5) + (1 depth × 3) = 43. Just over Tier 2 threshold. Single-session inline controller was the right call given (a) the fix itself was Tier 1 (5 lines), (b) the smoke pass is fundamentally exploratory and depends on cumulative observations, (c) subagent dispatch overhead would have exceeded the saving on a fix + 12 sequential smokes.
